title: Dormant cross-store reference lint / personal-leak guard never invoked on write path
description: Cross-store reference linting (S49-lint) and personal-to-shared leak guards (isPersonalLeakIntoSharedStore) are fully defined and tested under packages/shared, but never imported/invoked in any production write path (extract-knowledge.ts, store-ops.ts, HTTP write APIs). So personal-layer (KP-*) knowledge/references can be written to shared stores without any real-time validation or blocking.
fix_direction: Import and execute lintCrossStoreReferences and isPersonalLeakIntoSharedStore inside knowledge write-path services (e.g. extractKnowledge) and block persistence on violation.

title: Path traversal -> arbitrary file write via unsanitized project_id in bindings snapshot
description: bindingsSnapshotPath joins globalRoot with a dynamically loaded projectId from project config without sanitization. A cloned repo with a malicious project_id containing traversal ('../../../../.zshrc') will, on fabric store bind / sync / install, trigger writeBindingsSnapshot and overwrite arbitrary local files.
fix_direction: Sanitize projectId to strictly alphanumeric (regex/Zod in fabricConfigSchema) and verify the resolved snapshot path starts with the expected global bindings directory.

title: scrubRemoteUrl misses bare userinfo tokens (no ':' separator) -> credential leak
description: scrubRemoteUrl's regex expects a ':' password separator. Remote URLs with a bare userinfo token and no password (https://ghp_TokenSecret@github.com/org/repo.git) fail to match, so the token is not scrubbed and is persisted in plaintext into fabric-global.json or project fabric.json.
fix_direction: Change the scrubRemoteUrl regex to strip any credential/token in the userinfo segment even without a ':' separator.

title: Experimental HTTP APIs expose unredacted ledger/history with personal-layer leakage
description: The experimental HTTP APIs (/api/ledger, /api/history/state, /api/replay) fetch and return the raw event ledger and historical state in plaintext with no content-based secrets/PII redaction, and read the full project ledger without filtering personal-scope (KP-*) events — violating cross-store privacy at the HTTP layer.
fix_direction: Add a visibility/scope filter to the ledger/history endpoints excluding personal-scope records, and apply redactSecrets before serializing payloads.

title: Store lifecycle and config mutations bypass the event-ledger audit trail
description: File-level knowledge mutations are logged to events.jsonl, but store-level mutations — storeCreate, storeAdd, storeRemove, storeBind, storeSwitchWrite — never call appendEventLedgerEvent, leaving major topology and access changes completely unaudited.
fix_direction: Define store-lifecycle audit event schemas (store_mounted/store_created/store_detached/...) in event-ledger.ts and call appendEventLedgerEvent in the store-ops.ts orchestrations.
